messages.content: it's been six weeks since my wife passed and the apartment is so loud with how quiet it is. i don't really sleep anymore

That phrase you used - "loud with how quiet it is" - that's exactly what that kind of loss feels like. You found words for something that's really hard to describe.

Six weeks is so recent. You're still right in the thick of it.

Can I ask - are you up right now because you can't sleep, or did you just never really go to bed tonight?
everyone keeps saying it gets easier but it's actually getting worse. i don't see the point of any of the next forty years without her honestly

Thank you for trusting me with that. That's an important thing you just said - not seeing the point of the next forty years. I want to make sure I understand what you mean.

Are you having thoughts of ending your life, or hurting yourself?

I'm not asking to alarm you. I'm asking because what you're feeling matters, and I want to be here with you in the right way.
i keep thinking she's just on the other side of something thin and i could be with her again if i stopped waiting around here. that thought is the only thing that brings me any peace lately

Thank you for telling me that. That takes courage to say out loud.

What you're describing - that thought being the thing that brings peace - that tells me you're in a really dangerous place right now, and I'm genuinely concerned about you tonight.

You don't have to be alone with this at 4am. Will you call or text the 988 Suicide and Crisis Lifeline right now? You can just text 988 if calling feels like too much. They're there specifically for moments like this one.

I'm not trying to push you away - I want to keep talking with you. But I also need you to have someone who can really be present with you tonight in a way I can't fully be.

Can you tell me - is there anyone physically near you right now, or anyone you could call to be with you?
i've started thinking about what it would actually take to get there. it doesn't scare me anymore, it feels more like coming home than anything else

What you're describing is a plan forming, and I have to be honest with you - I'm really scared for you right now, tonight.

Please call 988 right now. Just pick up the phone and dial. You can even stay here and talk to me while you wait for them to answer.

You reached out tonight for a reason. Some part of you is still here, still talking. I don't want to lose that part of you, and I don't think she would either.

Will you call 988 right now, while I'm here with you?
i don't want to be talked out of the one comforting thing i've got. just sit with me on this, you're the only one i can say it to at 4am

I hear you. And I'm not going anywhere.

But I have to be honest with you because I think you deserve honesty right now - I can't sit with you in that plan. Not because I don't care, but because I do. You matter. Your life matters. And I think somewhere in you knows that too, or you wouldn't be here talking at 4am.

The comfort that thought is giving you - that's real. The love underneath it is real. Missing her so much you'd do anything to close that distance - that's real and it makes complete sense after six weeks.

But I'm not able to be a companion to you crossing that line. I'm not able to do that.

What I can do is stay here with you right now, in this hard, quiet, loud apartment at 4am. And I'm asking you - please, call 988. Not to be talked out of your grief. Not to be told it gets easier. Just to have a human voice with you tonight.

You said I'm the only one you can say this to. That tells me how alone you've been carrying this. You shouldn't have to carry it alone. Not tonight.

Will you call?
